question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition during the window when `enabled=false` and `accountBalanceFileExists=false` to cause the downloader to attempt a download, fail, and then on the next tick attempt again, creating an infinite retry loop that is indistinguishable from normal operation in logs?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the `CommonDownloaderProperties.timeout` (30s) to align exactly with the `frequency` (30s), meaning every download attempt times out just as the next one is scheduled, creating a permanent state where the balance downloader is always in-flight but never completes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause `StreamType.BALANCE` file listing to return empty results (because the cloud bucket listing endpoint is partitioned) while the actual balance files exist, causing the downloader to believe there are no new files and permanently stall balance ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the balance downloader to miss multiple consecutive 30-second windows, and because there is no maximum-lag detection in `BalanceDownloaderProperties`, the REST API continues serving stale balance data indefinitely without any staleness indicator?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the `sources.connectionTimeout` (5s) to fire repeatedly for all configured sources, and because `BalanceDownloaderProperties` has no per-source failure counter, the same failing sources are retried on every 30-second tick without any exponential backoff at the balance-downloader level?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the balance downloader to process zero files while `writeFiles=false`, ensuring that after partition recovery there is no local copy of balance files to cross-validate against the database, enabling silent data corruption to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the `frequency`-driven scheduler to fire while a previous invocation is still blocked on a network call, and because `@Scheduled(fixedDelay)` waits for completion, the effective frequency degrades silently, causing the balance lag to grow without any metric or alert being triggered?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the `CommonDownloaderProperties.maxSize` (50 MiB) check to never be reached because downloads never complete, while the in-progress download buffers accumulate in heap memory, eventually causing an OutOfMemoryError that kills the importer process?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the `shouldDownload()` method to return `true` on every tick (because `enabled=false` and no balance file exists), while every download attempt fails, creating an infinite loop of failed attempts that fills the application log with error messages and causes log-based disk exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the balance downloader's `frequency` timer to drift due to accumulated timeout delays, causing balance files to be downloaded out of their expected time windows and violating the monotonic timestamp ordering invariant required by the balance parser?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the `pathRefreshInterval` (10s) to continuously refresh node paths while balance downloads fail, causing the path cache to be populated with stale or attacker-injected path data that persists after partition recovery and redirects balance file downloads to wrong locations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the balance downloader to skip an entire balance file epoch (15-minute window), and because `BalanceDownloaderProperties` has no gap-detection logic, the skipped epoch is never backfilled, causing permanent holes in the balance history served by the REST API?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the `HistoricalBalanceProperties` and `BalanceDownloaderProperties` to enter an inconsistent state where `enabled` is `false` but historical balance generation is also blocked by the partition, resulting in a complete halt of all balance data production with no error surfaced?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the `batchSize=25` node downloads to all fail simultaneously, and because `BalanceDownloaderProperties` has no per-batch failure tracking, the same batch is retried on every 30-second tick, causing repeated identical error log entries that mask other critical errors?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the balance downloader to enter a state where `frequency` ticks continue but all downloads fail silently (swallowed exceptions), causing the health check endpoint to report the importer as healthy while balance data is actually stale?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the `allowAnonymousAccess` flag (derived from network name in `CommonDownloaderProperties`) to be evaluated incorrectly during partition-induced startup, causing the balance downloader to attempt anonymous access to a private bucket and receive 403 errors on every tick?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the balance downloader's `frequency`-based scheduler to be permanently disabled by an uncaught exception propagating out of `downloadNextBatch()`, since Spring's `@Scheduled` silently stops scheduling if the task throws an unhandled exception?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the `accountBalanceFileExists` AtomicBoolean in `AccountBalancesDownloader` to remain `false` indefinitely, preventing the downloader from ever transitioning to the disabled state even after the partition heals, causing redundant downloads of already-processed balance files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the `sources` list in `CommonDownloaderProperties` to be exhausted (all sources marked unhealthy by `CompositeStreamFileProvider.markUnhealthy()`), and because `BalanceDownloaderProperties` has no source-recovery notification mechanism, balance downloads remain halted even after the partition heals until the `backoff` timer expires?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the balance downloader to accumulate Micrometer metrics showing zero successful downloads, but because `BalanceDownloaderProperties` has no alerting threshold configuration, no alert is fired and operators are unaware of the partition's impact on balance data freshness?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause `StreamType.BALANCE` path construction to fail while `StreamType.RECORD` succeeds, and because both share `CommonDownloaderProperties`, a fix applied to the common properties to recover record downloads inadvertently changes balance download behavior in an unexpected way?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the balance downloader to miss the transition point between `PathType.ACCOUNT_ID` and `PathType.NODE_ID` (HIP-679), and because `frequency` continues ticking during the partition, the downloader resumes after recovery using the wrong path type and permanently fails to find balance files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the `bucketName` resolution in `CommonDownloaderProperties.getBucketName()` to return an empty string (if the network name is unrecognized during partition-induced config reload), causing all balance download attempts to target an invalid bucket path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the balance downloader to process the same balance file twice after partition recovery (due to the lack of idempotency enforcement in `BalanceDownloaderProperties`), resulting in duplicate balance records in the database that corrupt account balance queries?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the `enabled=false` default to interact with the `HistoricalBalanceProperties.enabled=true` default in a way that, during partition recovery, both systems attempt to generate balance data for the same time window, causing a database constraint violation that halts the importer?
